title: Cannot inspect image from atomic transport
body:
Copying image to atomic transport is okay:

```
[root@fedora ~]# skopeo copy docker-daemon:docker.io/busybox:latest atomic:docker-registry.engineering.redhat.com/gouyang/busybox:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:6a749002dd6a65988a6696ca4d0c4cbe87145df74e3bf6feae4025ab28f420f2
 1.28 MB / 1.28 MB [===========================================================]
Copying config sha256:54511612f1c4d97e93430fc3d5dc2f05dfbe8fb7e6259b7351deeca95eaf2971
 0 B / 1.46 KB [---------------------------------------------------------------]
Writing manifest to image destination
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```
Inspecting image from atomic transport is failed:

```
[root@fedora ~]# skopeo inspect atomic:docker-registry.engineering.redhat.com/gouyang/busybox:latest
FATA[0000] Get https://localhost:443/oapi/v1/namespaces/gouyang/imagestreams/busybox: dial tcp [::1]:443: getsockopt: connection refused 
```

comments.body:
Yeah; regardless of the hostname in the `atomic:` image reference, the implementation actually connects to the default cluster as configured in `~/.kube/config`.  If the two don’t match, things break.

Use `oc login` to log into `docker-registry.engineering.redhat.com`, or perhaps `oc config use-context`.

After oc login, it does work.

```
# skopeo inspect atomic:docker-registry.engineering.redhat.com/gouyang/busybox:latest
{
    "Tag": "latest",
    "Digest": "sha256:c70a92381fe213de9086758c3ea5813dfb7e96a2b6b75d276b7b9ac8f5809097",
    "RepoTags": [],
    "Created": "2017-09-13T10:14:16.620085795Z",
    "DockerVersion": "17.06.2-ce",
    "Labels": null,
    "Architecture": "amd64",
    "Os": "linux",
    "Layers": [
        "sha256:03b1be98f3f9b05cb57782a3a71a44aaf6ec695de5f4f8e6c1058cd42f04953e",
        "sha256:a3ed95caeb02ffe68cdd9fd84406680ae93d633cb16422d00e8a7c22955b46d4"
    ]
}
```
